content.problemStatement:
Nayra doesn't like stories of people receiving random trees as birthday presents, but this time she received a tree as a present for her own birthday! After struggling for a day trying to figure out what to do with this tree, she asked Aryan for help. He gave her this problem.

You are given a tree with NNN vertices and weighted edges. It is rooted at vertex 111.

Define g(u,v)g(u,v)g(u,v) to be the maximum weight of an edge on the shortest path between vertex uuu and vertex vvv in this tree.

Just calculating g(u,v)g(u,v)g(u,v) is too easy, so you have to process QQQ queries.

In each query, you are given KKK **distinct** vertices v1,v2,‚Ä¶,vKv_1, v_2, \ldots, v_Kv1‚Äã,v2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,vK‚Äã. You have to compute the sum of the ggg-values of each pair of vertices among these KKK, i.e, the value

‚àëi=1K‚àí1‚àëj=i+1Kg(vi,vj)\sum_{i=1}^{K-1} \sum_{j=i+1}^K g(v_i, v_j)‚àëi=1K‚àí1‚Äã‚àëj=i+1K‚Äãg(vi‚Äã,vj‚Äã)

**Note**: The input and output are large, so use fast input-output methods.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and QQQ, denoting the number of vertices in the tree and the number of queries respectively.
The second line contains N‚àí1N-1N‚àí1 space-separated integers P2,P3,‚Ä¶,PNP_2, P_3, \dots, P_NP2‚Äã,P3‚Äã,‚Ä¶,PN‚Äã, where PiP_iPi‚Äã is the parent of vertex iii.
The third line contains N‚àí1N-1N‚àí1 space-separated integers W2,W3,‚Ä¶,WNW_2, W_3, \dots, W_NW2‚Äã,W3‚Äã,‚Ä¶,WN‚Äã, where WiW_iWi‚Äã is the weight of the edge between vertices iii and PiP_iPi‚Äã.
The next QQQ lines contain queries you will have to answer. The description of QQQ queries follows.
The iii-th line contains an integer KiK_iKi‚Äã followed by KiK_iKi‚Äã distinct space-separated integers v1,v2,‚Ä¶,vKiv_1, v_2, \ldots, v_{K_i}v1‚Äã,v2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,vKi‚Äã‚Äã, where KiK_iKi‚Äã is the number of vertices in this query and v1,v2,‚Ä¶vKiv_1, v_2, \ldots v_{K_i}v1‚Äã,v2‚Äã,‚Ä¶vKi‚Äã‚Äã denote the vertices themselves.


Output Format
For each test, print one line containing QQQ space-separated integers. The iii-th of these integers should be the answer to the iii-th query.
constraints: All viv_ivi‚Äã present in a single query are distinct
It is guaranteed that the sum of KiK_iKi‚Äã over all queries over all test cases in a single test file does not exceed 1.5‚ãÖ1061.5 \cdot 10^61.5‚ãÖ106
It is guaranteed that the sum of QQQ over all test cases in a single test file does not exceed 3‚ãÖ1043 \cdot 10^43‚ãÖ104

Explanation:
In the second sample:
Let's look at different values of g(u,v)g(u,v)g(u,v) for this tree.
For the first query, g(1,4)=40g(1,4) = 40g(1,4)=40, g(1,5)=50g(1,5) = 50g(1,5)=50, g(1,6)=60g(1,6) = 60g(1,6)=60, g(4,5)=50g(4,5) = 50g(4,5)=50, g(4,6)=60g(4,6) = 60g(4,6)=60, g(5,6)=60g(5,6) = 60g(5,6)=60. The sum of all these values is 320320320.
For the second query, g(2,3)=30g(2,3) = 30g(2,3)=30, g(2,7)=70g(2,7) = 70g(2,7)=70, g(2,8)=80g(2,8) = 80g(2,8)=80, g(3,7)=70g(3,7) = 70g(3,7)=70, g(3,8)=80g(3,8) = 80g(3,8)=80, g(7,8)=80g(7,8) = 80g(7,8)=80. The sum of all these values is 410410410.
Note: The second sample does not satisfy the constraint Ki‚â•10K_i \geq 10Ki‚Äã‚â•10, so it won't be present in the actual test data. It's added here only for illustration.
